title: Add a new widget to change the template to display hits
body:
I'd need a new widget to change the template used to display hits on the page. Small parenthesis, for now, I made a custom system, but I encounter an issue with the "**currentRefinedValues**" widget. To change the mode of displayal, I change the template in the "**hits**" widget, and I call a `search.start();` This works well, but after a "clear filter", when I call the `search.start();`, some facets are reappearing in the "**currentRefinedValues**" widget (as if they were still in the object memory). As this feature could be usefull to someone else, I prefer suggest to add the widget instead of correcting a very specific bug. End of the parenthesis. The expected result could be something like this : ![capture du 2016-02-15 15 17 56](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/293127/13051181/14feba06-d3f8-11e5-870a-d45632175f1f.png) # API ``` search.addWidget( instantsearch.widgets.hitsDisplayMode({ container: '#hits-display-mode', templates: { small: '
Hit {{objectID}}
', medium: '
Hit {{objectID}}: {{{_highlightResult.name.value}}}
', full: '

Hit {{objectID}}

{{{_highlightResult.name.value}}}
' }, default: 'medium', cssClasses: { active: 'active', iconSmall :'fa-th', iconMedium :'fa-bars', iconFull :'fa-th-large', small: 'small', medium: 'medium', full: 'full' } }) ); ``` The requirements will be the small, medium and full template. Maybe we could use the template specified in "**hits**" as the medium one, I don't know. We could also specify css classes, and the default template to apply (but this would be in conflict with the "**hits**" widget again, as they are complementary ) The rended html could be something like this : ```
``` # TODO - [ ] find a cool name for the widget - [ ] determine the better way to do that without conflicting with the "hits" widget - [ ] affine the customizable options (css, templating) Thanks a lot
